title: eriogonum proliferum subsp. anserinum
intro: Eriogonum proliferum subsp. anserinum, also known as Desert Birch or Mormon Tea, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and acting as a natural diuretic. Desert Birch can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
